message: Per-document Matthew category scores are not yet included in this site. This tab shows the planned crosswalk for comparing the broad category pipeline with the v3 provision-level pipeline.
needed_file: matthew_category_outputs.csv with document_id, category, category_present, chunk_count, category_summary, elo_score or standardized_score, and run_id.

rows.matthew_object: Bargaining-unit recognition, scope, union security, and coverage language
rows.note: Mostly context in v3 unless tied to worker-facing scope or enforceable access.

rows.matthew_object: Agreement duration, expiration, reopening, and renewal terms
rows.note: No stable v3 generosity concept yet.

rows.matthew_object: Management rights, retained discretion, and employer authority
rows.note: Needs decomposition because broad management-rights clauses can reduce generosity in several domains.

rows.matthew_object: Wages, pay increases, wage schedules, premiums, allowances, and pay-related provisions
rows.comparison_level: Presence, relative metrics, and local scores
rows.note: Direct comparison should separate base wages, growth, and premium rules.

rows.matthew_object: Regular hours, schedule notice, rest periods, overtime assignment, cancellations, and workload timing
rows.comparison_level: Presence/profile and local scores where available
rows.note: Some scheduling protections need common-unit rules or sector-specific anchors.

rows.matthew_object: Vacation, sick, holidays, parental/family, personal, civic, union, and other leave
rows.note: Good overlap category, though v3 avoids rewarding documents merely for naming many leave types.

rows.matthew_object: Active health benefits, plan design, employee contributions, retiree health, dental, vision, life, disability
rows.comparison_level: Presence, local scores, and relative/proxy metrics
rows.note: Direct comparison must separate contribution burden from plan design and external plan references.

rows.matthew_object: Non-health benefits such as pension, annuity, training funds, child care, legal, and other welfare funds
rows.note: Many values are external-fund proxies rather than directly comparable worker benefits.

rows.matthew_object: Layoff, recall, severance, seniority, subcontracting, work preservation, and job-security protections
rows.comparison_level: Presence and local scores where available
rows.note: Good overlap, but v3 splits security into distinct worker-protection mechanisms.

rows.matthew_object: Training, tuition, certification, apprenticeship, and professional development
rows.note: Currently more profile than score-ready in v3.

rows.matthew_object: Grievance, arbitration, discipline, appeal, just cause, and dispute-resolution rights
rows.note: Strong overlap category; v3 can explain whether generosity comes from access, timelines, representation, or arbitration.

rows.matthew_object: PPE, unsafe-work rights, safety committees, violence/assault, hazard response, and safety standards
rows.comparison_level: Presence/profile and local scores where available
rows.note: Needs care because high safety text can reflect job hazard exposure, not only more generous protection.

rows.matthew_object: Other provisions not assigned to a main category
rows.note: Useful for discovering missing concepts, not for direct generosity comparison.
